text: There once was an owl whom folks wouldn used think much of. Late-night hours he'd hunt. Strange things, he'd find. Frogs and mice, that he'd grind. Owls don't talk much these days. Just owl stuff they'll say. But somehow I think they know. Our owl did know, quite a flair. With wings out stretched. He'd swoop like aerial rail. They say he saved little bunny. That's all the story there. Poor bunny never spoke. Just hop now.
